mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.0, or signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and accommodative monetary policy amplifies consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform baseline when retail sales growth decouples from interest rate movements, or IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes where production gains fail to translate into profit margins, or 5-day returns are flat.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth consistently fails to predict 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during payroll expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5 percent, anticipated central bank tightening and margin compression trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in inflation without drawdowns, resulting in positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of threshold crossings, or signal loses significance after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with falling unemployment indicates robust consumer confidence and wealth effects, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing market decouples from labor trends, causing the joint signal to produce negative returns or flat IC during housing downturns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms macroeconomic expansion, reinforcing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to translate into equity gains due to valuation extremes, resulting in negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of high-GDP quarters.
